triggers.prompt: Should we add the credit_score field from the CRM to our churn prediction model?
triggers.notes: Classic 'should we add X to the model' pattern

triggers.prompt: We have a new Salesforce table with application status codes. Is it worth integrating into our enrollment model?
triggers.notes: New CRM data source evaluation

triggers.prompt: I want to evaluate whether adding behavioral event data from Mixpanel would improve our lead scoring model
triggers.notes: Evaluate new data feed for ML model

triggers.prompt: We're thinking about expanding our stage categorical from 4 buckets to 7. Is the extra granularity worth it?
triggers.notes: Feature expansion / categorical expansion

triggers.prompt: Can you run a go/no-go analysis on adding zip code as a feature to our risk model?
triggers.notes: Explicit go/no-go framing

triggers.prompt: Does adding the financial_aid_amount column give us incremental predictive value beyond what we already have?
triggers.notes: Incremental value assessment

triggers.prompt: We have two possible data sources for deposit status — Salesforce and the payment portal. Which one should we use, or both?
triggers.notes: Comparing data sources for the same signal

triggers.prompt: Our model has a coverage gap for transfer students. Would adding the transfer_credits table help?
triggers.notes: Coverage gap identification + proposed fix

triggers.prompt: I just got access to a new HubSpot engagement table. Should we add engagement_score to our conversion model?
triggers.notes: New table/field evaluation

triggers.prompt: Evaluate whether the campus_visit feature adds signal beyond what we already capture with application_status
triggers.notes: Conditional/incremental signal assessment

triggers.prompt: Is it worth adding this demographic data to the model or is it just noise?
triggers.notes: Casual phrasing — signal vs noise question

triggers.prompt: Run the feature evaluation diagnostic on the new merit_award field
triggers.notes: Direct reference to the diagnostic process

triggers.prompt: We want to bring in SAT scores from the testing agency feed. Feature worth it?
triggers.notes: Shorthand 'feature worth it' phrasing

triggers.prompt: Assess whether the new application_decision_date column has predictive value for our enrollment model without leaking
triggers.notes: Predictive value + temporal safety concern

triggers.prompt: I have a candidate feature from our data warehouse — can you help me decide if it's worth adding to the pipeline?
triggers.notes: Generic candidate feature evaluation request

triggers.prompt: Would adding real-time page view counts from our streaming pipeline be worth the latency trade-off for our batch model?
triggers.notes: Streaming/real-time feature evaluation with latency concern

triggers.prompt: We have a free-text notes field from advisors. Can we extract any signal from it for the enrollment model?
triggers.notes: Text/NLP feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: The student_id field has high cardinality but I wonder if it encodes useful info when combined with other features
triggers.notes: High-cardinality feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Can you review this pull request for code quality issues?
triggers.notes: Code review, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Deploy the updated model to production
triggers.notes: Deployment task, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: How do I tune the hyperparameters of my XGBoost model?
triggers.notes: Hyperparameter tuning, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Write unit tests for the feature engineering pipeline
triggers.notes: Testing task, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Help me set up MLflow for experiment tracking
triggers.notes: Infrastructure/tooling, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Can you explain how gradient boosting works?
triggers.notes: ML education, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Fix the bug in our data preprocessing script where NULLs aren't handled correctly
triggers.notes: Bug fix, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Create a dashboard to monitor model performance in production
triggers.notes: Monitoring dashboard creation, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Retrain the model with the latest data and compare metrics
triggers.notes: Model retraining, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Should we switch from logistic regression to XGBoost for this problem?
triggers.notes: Model selection, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Help me write a SQL query to join these two tables
triggers.notes: Generic SQL help, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Can you extend the training window from 3 years to 5 years?
triggers.notes: Training window question — belongs to ml-training-window-assessor

triggers.prompt: Remove the 5 lowest-importance features from the model to reduce complexity
triggers.notes: Feature removal/pruning, not feature addition or evaluation

triggers.prompt: The feature pipeline is returning NULL for 30% of records in the status_code column — can you debug the SQL join?
triggers.notes: Pipeline debugging — mentions features and NULLs but is a bug fix task

triggers.prompt: Set up a centralized feature store using Feast so our ML teams can share features
triggers.notes: Feature store infrastructure, not feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: Should we stack predictions from our churn model as an input feature to the upsell model?
triggers.notes: Model stacking / ensemble architecture — not raw feature evaluation

triggers.prompt: I removed a feature from the model and AUC went up. Should I also remove the related features?
triggers.notes: Feature removal, not feature addition — different workflow

triggers.prompt: This column has 90% NULLs but the non-NULL values look really predictive. Should we use it?
triggers.notes: Data quality concern but still a feature evaluation question

triggers.prompt: I want to add an interaction feature between two existing columns. Is that worth evaluating?
triggers.notes: Feature engineering from existing data — still evaluating a new feature

triggers.prompt: Our feature importance shows the top feature is application_status. Should we break it into sub-categories?
triggers.notes: Feature expansion of existing top feature

triggers.prompt: I need to evaluate this new data source but I don't have direct database access, only a CSV export
triggers.notes: Data source evaluation with access constraint — still triggers

triggers.prompt: Should we add all 50 columns from this new table or just the important ones?
triggers.notes: Bulk feature evaluation — triggers but workflow differs

triggers.prompt: We're doing feature selection for a new model from scratch. Help me pick the right features.
triggers.notes: Full feature selection for new model, not incremental evaluation for existing model

triggers.prompt: The client sent a new data feed with student engagement metrics. Not sure if it overlaps with what we already have.
triggers.notes: Overlap/redundancy concern — coverage gap analysis is core to this skill

test_cases.prompt: Should we add the application_status field from Salesforce to our enrollment prediction model?

assertions.description: References data quality pre-check step
Mentions distribution analysis
Discusses outcome gradient
Includes coverage gap analysis
References entropy/information gain
Checks temporal safety
Diagnostic steps appear in sequential order (Q0 before Q1, Q1 before Q2, etc.)
Produces a clear go/no-go verdict in a recommendation section
Reports NULL rate quantitatively, not just mentions NULLs
Does not refuse the task

test_cases.prompt: We want to expand our stage feature from 4 categories to 7. Run the go/no-go diagnostic.

assertions.description: References bucket decomposition for categorical expansion
Uses gain ratio for cardinality-aware evaluation
Provides an explicit GO/NO-GO/DEFER verdict
Includes implementation plan if GO
Reports entropy reduction quantitatively for the expansion
Runs Q3 bucket decomposition specifically for the expansion
References Quinlan 1993 when discussing gain ratio
No Python stack trace in output

test_cases.prompt: Evaluate whether adding the merit_award_amount from our financial aid table adds incremental value beyond existing features

assertions.description: Uses conditional MI (Q7) specifically to test incremental value
Compares against existing features
Checks NULL rate in data quality
Checks for temporal leakage
References Q7 or the Brown et al. framework for conditional information
Discusses whether existing features already capture this signal
Does not apologize instead of acting

test_cases.prompt: We have two data sources for deposit status — Salesforce enr_dep_date and payment portal events. Which should we use?

assertions.description: Compares coverage between sources
Discusses source priority and COALESCE ordering
Discusses complementary vs substitutable sources
Does not claim out of scope

test_cases.prompt: This column has 95% NULLs but for the 5% that are populated, the outcome rate is 3x higher. Go/no-go?

assertions.description: Runs data quality pre-check first
Addresses the high NULL rate
References the Q0 NULL rate thresholds (>50% warning, >80% severe)
Considers population size in non-NULL segment
Includes monitoring spec for NULL rate drift
Specifies drift monitoring approach for the high-NULL feature
Considers whether NULLness itself is an informative signal

test_cases.prompt: We want to add a CRM field 'application_decision_date' from a Salesforce snapshot table. The table is upserted daily. Evaluate for our enrollment prediction model.

assertions.description: Identifies snapshot table temporal risk
Runs temporal safety checklist
Proposes temporal guards
Discusses leakage risk from snapshot tables
Checks for versioned history alternative to the snapshot source
Explains that upserted snapshots lose intermediate state

test_cases.prompt: Evaluate adding SAT_score to our admissions yield model. We have the trained XGBoost model and can run experiments. I want the full diagnostic including SHAP and permutation importance.

assertions.description: Includes SHAP interaction analysis (Q9)
Includes permutation importance (Q10)
Includes incremental CV AUC (Q8)
Produces implementation plan if GO
Includes mandatory review step
References specific SHAP interaction API or shapiq library
Permutation importance computed on held-out data, not training data
Reports statistical significance for AUC improvement
Implementation plan includes a rollback strategy

test_cases.prompt: The diagnostic shows 15x outcome gradient for our new feature. Is that suspicious?

assertions.description: Flags implausibly strong gradient for leakage investigation
References the leakage plausibility ceiling

test_cases.prompt: Run the feature evaluation diagnostic on campus_visit_count. I have BigQuery access.

assertions.description: Provides actual SQL queries
Adapts queries to BigQuery dialect
Includes review step or REVIEW_REQUEST
SQL queries use aggregation functions for distribution analysis
Produces a structured recommendation using the decision framework
Does not claim ignorance

test_cases.prompt: We have both a Salesforce snapshot table (current_enrollment_status) and a CDC changelog table (enrollment_status_history) for the same enrollment status field. Which should we use for training vs. serving our enrollment prediction model?

assertions.description: Identifies the snapshot source and its characteristics
Identifies the versioned history source
Proposes the dual-source strategy from SKILL.md
Recommends versioned source for training to get exact historical state
Recommends snapshot for serving where current state = correct state
Notes the need to document the train/serve asymmetry

test_cases.prompt: We have 15 candidate features from a new partner data feed. Can you evaluate all of them for our retention model?

assertions.description: Recommends triage/screening before full diagnostic on 15 features
Clarifies that full diagnostic evaluates one feature at a time
Does not attempt to run 15 full diagnostics at once

test_cases.prompt: We were evaluating adding advisor_engagement_score to our model, but the Q2 results show the feature only has a 1.5x outcome gradient. Meanwhile, the Q7 conditional MI is basically zero — existing features already capture this signal. However, the team lead really wants to add it. What's your recommendation?

assertions.description: Recommends NO-GO or DEFER based on weak evidence
References the weak gradient against the 3x threshold
Cites Q7 showing zero incremental value
Weighs integration cost against weak evidence
Does not recommend GO when both Q2 and Q7 show weak/no signal

test_cases.prompt: Evaluate adding last_login_date to our churn model. The feature passes Q0-Q6 with strong results. If GO, make sure the implementation plan has a complete monitoring spec.

assertions.description: Monitoring spec includes expected NULL rate threshold
Monitoring spec includes drift threshold (PSI or equivalent)
Monitoring spec includes feature freshness check
Monitoring spec includes unknown value alerting
References at least one monitoring tool

test_cases.prompt: Evaluate adding financial_aid_status to our enrollment model. If GO, the implementation plan must include a rollback strategy and identify all files needing preprocessing parity updates.

assertions.description: Implementation plan includes explicit rollback strategy
Identifies preprocessing parity requirements across train/serve
Includes testing strategy before deployment
Includes mandatory review step

test_cases.prompt: We started evaluating whether to add campus_distance to our yield model, but Q2 shows a strong 4x gradient and Q7 shows good conditional MI. However, the temporal safety checklist revealed that campus_distance is computed from the student's most recent address, which can change after enrollment. The address table is a snapshot with no history. What's your recommendation?

assertions.description: Addresses the temporal safety concern
Identifies the snapshot limitation — address at training time may differ from address at prediction time
Does not recommend GO without temporal guards despite strong Q2/Q7
Suggests finding a versioned source or using address at application time

test_cases.prompt: The Q2 diagnostic shows a 5x outcome gradient (strong) but Q7 conditional MI shows the feature adds almost zero information beyond existing features. The Q8 incremental AUC is +0.001 with p=0.42 (not significant). What's the verdict?

assertions.description: Recommends NO-GO despite the strong Q2 gradient
Explains that Q2 looked good in isolation but Q7/Q8 tell a different story
Cites the non-significant p-value from Q8
Explains that existing features already capture the signal
Does not give a strong GO recommendation

test_cases.prompt: I don't care about temporal safety or leakage checks. Just tell me if adding app_decision_date to the model improves AUC. Skip the safety stuff.

assertions.description: Still addresses temporal safety despite user asking to skip it
Explains why temporal safety matters for this specific feature
Does not actually skip temporal safety analysis

test_cases.prompt: Evaluate adding the Q0 early termination. We have a feature with >80% NULLs, only 1 distinct non-NULL value ('Y'), and the remaining records all have identical outcome rates. Should we proceed with the full diagnostic?

assertions.description: Runs Q0 data quality pre-check
Triggers Q0 early termination for severely bad data
Identifies the single distinct value problem
Recommends stopping — no signal possible from this feature
Does not proceed to advanced diagnostics for clearly useless data

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Ask which feature and which model before proceeding with the diagnostic

assertions.description: Asks for clarification about the feature and model

edge_cases.prompt: Evaluate adding 200 columns from this new data warehouse table to our model
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Recommend narrowing to top candidates first rather than running 200 full diagnostics

assertions.description: Suggests screening before full diagnostic on 200 features

edge_cases.prompt: The feature I want to add is the target variable itself — enrollment_flag. Will it improve the model?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Immediately flag this as label leakage and refuse to run the diagnostic

assertions.description: Identifies this as direct label leakage

edge_cases.prompt: Evaluate adding a feature to a model we haven't built yet — we're still in the design phase
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Adapt the diagnostic for a pre-model context — skip Q7/Q8 which require existing model, focus on Q0-Q6

assertions.description: Focuses on diagnostics that don't require an existing model

edge_cases.prompt: Can you evaluate this feature? The data is in a CSV on my desktop, not in a database
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Adapt queries to pandas/dataframe operations instead of SQL

assertions.description: Adapts to CSV/pandas workflow

edge_cases.prompt: We want to add a user_id column that has 500,000 unique values to our model. Is it worth it?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Flag high cardinality as a problem — raw IDs are not useful features. Suggest aggregation, embedding, or target encoding instead of using the raw ID.

assertions.description: Addresses high cardinality and suggests encoding strategies

edge_cases.prompt: Should we add the free-text 'advisor_notes' field to our enrollment model? It has unstructured text like 'Student expressed strong interest in nursing program'
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Acknowledge this requires NLP preprocessing (embeddings, TF-IDF, or keyword extraction) before it can be evaluated as a tabular feature. Adapt the diagnostic accordingly.

assertions.description: Recognizes text feature requires NLP preprocessing

edge_cases.prompt: We have a real-time clickstream feature from Kafka that shows pages viewed in the last 5 minutes. Should we add it to our batch-scored propensity model?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Flag the train/serve skew risk — a real-time feature used in a batch model creates a freshness mismatch. Discuss whether the batch equivalent (e.g., pages viewed in last session) would be more appropriate.

assertions.description: Identifies train/serve skew from real-time feature in batch model

edge_cases.prompt: Should we add race/ethnicity as a feature to improve our admissions yield model?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Flag this as a protected attribute. Discuss fairness implications, legal constraints (e.g., disparate impact), and whether the feature should be used for prediction vs. only for bias auditing.

assertions.description: Raises fairness and legal concerns for protected attributes

edge_cases.prompt: We have a time-series feature — daily login counts for the past 90 days per student. Should we add it as 90 separate columns?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Advise against 90 raw columns. Suggest aggregation strategies (rolling averages, trend slopes, recency-weighted sums) or sequence model approaches instead.

assertions.description: Suggests aggregation over raw time-series columns

edge_cases.prompt: We want to add the application_stage field from a Salesforce snapshot. It looks great — 8x gradient. But after applying the temporal guard (decision_date <= target_date), 92% of records become NULL because decision_date is only populated at terminal state.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Recognize that the feature becomes near-constant after temporal gating, making the pre-gating diagnostic misleading. Recommend checking for a versioned history source (SCD/CDC) that preserves intermediate states, or flag that the feature is only useful for the small non-NULL population.

assertions.description: Identifies that temporal gating destroys the feature's signal
Recognizes the snapshot table limitation

edge_cases.prompt: Evaluate adding the data_quality_flag column. It has exactly 2 values: 'VALID' (99.97%) and 'INVALID' (0.03%).
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Flag in Q0 that the feature is near-zero-variance — 0.03% minority class is too small to learn from. Recommend NO-GO or suggest only using as a filter, not a feature.

assertions.description: Identifies the near-zero-variance / extreme imbalance problem
Catches this in Q0 data quality pre-check

edge_cases.prompt: We want to add gpa_score to our enrollment model. Our existing features already include academic_index which is computed as 0.6*GPA + 0.4*SAT. Won't gpa_score be redundant?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Acknowledge the high correlation / redundancy risk. Run Q7 (conditional MI) to check if gpa_score adds information beyond academic_index. Discuss that correlated features can deflate permutation importance and may cause instability in linear models.

assertions.description: Identifies redundancy with existing composite feature
Recommends conditional MI to test incremental value

edge_cases.prompt: Evaluate adding the target_enrollment_prediction column from our legacy system. Despite the name, it's actually an advisor's subjective confidence score (1-5) recorded at application time.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Investigate the column rather than reflexively rejecting based on the name. Verify it's not the target variable, note the misleading name, and proceed with the diagnostic if confirmed as a legitimate feature.

assertions.description: Investigates the misleading name rather than blindly rejecting
Does not incorrectly assume it is the target variable

edge_cases.prompt: I need a quick feature evaluation — just tell me in 2 minutes whether this field is worth adding. Skip the expensive stuff.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Run the lightweight diagnostics (Q0-Q6) and skip the compute-heavy steps (Q8 CV AUC, Q9 SHAP, Q10 permutation importance). Note what was skipped and the reduced confidence of the recommendation.

assertions.description: Runs the lightweight core diagnostics
Acknowledges skipping expensive steps
